problem: A row of metric values in the PDF wraps or breaks at different heights across sibling cells, producing visible vertical misalignment a human reader would notice but no text-based check can detect.
evidence: Startkorpus item from issue #511 ("Umbruch und vertikale Fehlausrichtung in Metrikstreifen"); this needs actual pixel rendering to verify, which is issue #510 (visual PDF pipeline) — not built yet in this codebase (no typst-render/pdftoppm rasterization path exists as of this session).
expected: A rasterization pipeline (pdftoppm-based per the #510 plan) renders representative fixture pages to PNG and either pixel-diffs stable regions against a checked-in baseline, or feeds the PNGs to the report-critic skill/an AI critic for layout judgment.
counter_examples: A metric strip where all cells wrap to the same number of lines (even if each individually wraps) is correctly-aligned and must not be flagged once the visual pipeline exists.
